tool.name: TSENAT

regex.description: An undefined error occurred, please check your input carefully and contact your administrator.
An undefined error occurred, please check your input carefully and contact your administrator.

tool.version_command: echo $(R --version | grep version | head -n 1) && Rscript -e "library('TSENAT'); cat('TSENAT', as.character(packageVersion('TSENAT')), sep='\\n')"
tool.command:
#if str($salmon_input.input_type) == 'individual' #set $input_files = $salmon_input.salmon_files #else #set $input_files = $salmon_input.salmon_collection #end if
mkdir -p './salmon_input' && #for $f in $input_files #set $sample_id = str($f.element_identifier).replace('.sf', '') mkdir -p './salmon_input/${sample_id}' && ln -sf '${f}' './salmon_input/${sample_id}/quant.sf' && #end for
## Handle annotation file - create symlink with proper extension for gzipped files #if $annotation.is_of_type("gff3.gz"): ln -s '${annotation}' './annotation.gff3.gz' && #set $annotation_path = './annotation.gff3.gz' #else ln -s '${annotation}' './annotation.gff3' && #set $annotation_path = './annotation.gff3' #end if
Rscript '${__tool_directory__}/tsenat.R' --salmon_dir './salmon_input' --metadata '${sample_sheet}' --annotation '${annotation_path}' --sample_col '${sample_col}' --condition_col '${condition_col}' #if str($paired_design.paired) == 'yes' --subject_col '${paired_design.subject_col}' --paired #end if --control '${control}' #if str($advanced_options.enabled) == 'yes' --q_min $advanced_options.q_parameters.q_min --q_max $advanced_options.q_parameters.q_max --q_step $advanced_options.q_parameters.q_step #end if --filter_stringency '${data_filtering.filter_stringency}' #if str($normalization_options.norm) == 'true' --norm #end if #if str($data_filtering.min_tpm) != '' --min_tpm $data_filtering.min_tpm #end if #if str($data_filtering.min_isoform_abundance) != '' --min_isoform_abundance $data_filtering.min_isoform_abundance #end if #if str($normalization_options.norm_method) != 'none' --norm_method '${normalization_options.norm_method}' #end if #if str($normalization_options.pseudocount) != '' --pseudocount $normalization_options.pseudocount #end if --shrinkage '${normalization_options.shrinkage}' --min_valid_frac $normalization_options.min_valid_frac #if str($normalization_options.reference_group) != '' --reference_group '${normalization_options.reference_group}' #end if #if str($normalization_options.what_output) != 'S' --what '${normalization_options.what_output}' #end if #if str($bootstrap_options.bootstrap) == 'true' --bootstrap #end if --nboot $bootstrap_options.nboot --bootstrap_method '${bootstrap_options.bootstrap_method}' --bootstrap_ci $bootstrap_options.bootstrap_ci #if str($bootstrap_options.bootstrap_include_diagnostics) == 'true' --bootstrap_include_diagnostics #end if --sait_method '${sait_testing.sait_method}' --sait_pcorr '${sait_testing.sait_pcorr}' #if str($sait_testing.sait_method) == 'gee' and str($sait_testing.gee_conditional.show_gee) == 'yes' --corstr '${sait_testing.gee_conditional.gee_corstr}' #end if #if str($sait_testing.multicorr_method) != 'default' --multicorr '${sait_testing.multicorr_method}' #end if #if $scheirer_ray_hare.srh_advanced #if str($scheirer_ray_hare.srh_options.configure_srh) == 'yes' --wy_randomizations $scheirer_ray_hare.srh_options.wy_randomizations --eta2_threshold_moderate $scheirer_ray_hare.srh_options.eta2_threshold_moderate --eta2_threshold_strong $scheirer_ray_hare.srh_options.eta2_threshold_strong --min_nperm $scheirer_ray_hare.srh_options.min_nperm --max_nperm $scheirer_ray_hare.srh_options.max_nperm #end if #end if #if str($jackknife_switching.jis_use_sait_fdr) == 'true' --sait_jis_fdr #end if #if $jackknife_switching.jis_advanced #if str($jackknife_switching.jis_options.configure_jis) == 'yes' --jis_sait_p_threshold $jackknife_switching.jis_options.jis_sait_p_threshold --jis_threshold $jackknife_switching.jis_options.jis_threshold #end if #end if --p_threshold $statistical_thresholds.p_threshold --fdr_threshold $statistical_thresholds.fdr_threshold --significance_threshold $statistical_thresholds.significance_threshold --divergence_ci $divergence_testing.divergence_ci --assumptions_checks 'all' --nthreads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2}" --output_prefix 'tsenat' #if str($advanced_options.enabled) == 'yes' #if str($advanced_options.skip_unmapped) == 'true' --skip_unmapped #end if #if $advanced_options.save_intermediates --save_intermediates #end if #end if --output_dir './'

param.name: input_type
param.label: Salmon input format
param.help: Choose how to provide Salmon quantification files: individual files or a dataset collection

param.name: salmon_files
param.label: Salmon quantification files (.sf)
param.help: Select individual Salmon .sf quantification files. File names must match sample IDs in the sample sheet. Format: tab-delimited with columns: Name, Length, EffectiveLength, TPM, NumReads.

param.name: salmon_collection
param.label: Salmon dataset collection
param.help: Select a dataset collection containing Salmon .sf files. Element names must match sample IDs in the sample sheet.

param.name: sample_sheet
param.label: Sample sheet (metadata file)
param.help: Tab-separated file with sample information. Must contain columns for sample IDs, experimental conditions, and optionally subject/pairing information. See help section for format details.

param.name: annotation
param.label: Transcript annotation
param.help: GFF3 annotation file with transcript ID to gene ID mapping (e.g., GENCODE GFF3). Used to group transcripts by gene and map annotation information.

param.name: sample_col
param.label: Sample ID column
param.help: Select column containing sample identifiers. Values must match Salmon file names (element identifiers if using collection).

param.name: condition_col
param.label: Condition column
param.help: Select column containing experimental conditions/treatment groups (e.g., normal, tumor, control, treatment).

param.name: control
param.label: Reference condition
param.help: Reference/control condition value for contrast specification. Must match a value in the condition column. Typically the untreated or normal condition.

param.name: subject_col
param.label: Subject/pairing column
param.help: Select column grouping paired or repeated samples (e.g., patient ID, animal ID). Each unique value represents one subject with multiple samples.

param.name: filter_stringency
param.label: Low-abundance filter stringency
param.help: Filter strategy for low-abundance transcripts. Soft: ≥25% presence in samples (preserve isoform diversity). Medium: ≥50% presence with TPM above median (balances noise reduction and diversity, recommended). Severe: ≥75% presence with high TPM (focus on abundant isoforms only). Choice affects entropy estimates: severe reduces noise but may miss rare isoforms and reduce diversity signal.

param.name: min_tpm
param.label: Minimum TPM threshold
param.help: (Optional) Minimum TPM value for transcript filtering (default: 1.0 if not specified). Transcripts with TPM < threshold in samples below min_samples are excluded. Leave empty to use default.

param.name: min_isoform_abundance
param.label: Minimum isoform abundance within gene
param.help: (Optional) Relative abundance threshold for isoforms within each gene (0-1 scale). Default: 0.05 (5%). Set to 0 to skip per-isoform filtering. For example, 0.05 means retain isoforms comprising >5% of the gene's total expression.

param.name: norm
param.label: Enable normalization
param.help: Normalize entropy estimates to [0,1] range for comparable cross-q assessment. Recommended (default: enabled) for comparing entropy across different q-values and between genes. Disable only if raw unnormalized entropy is needed.

param.name: norm_method
param.label: Normalization method
param.help: Method for normalization. Default (automatic): chooses algorithm based on data. Range: [0,1] scaling. Z-score: centered and scaled. Quantile: quantile normalization for cross-sample comparison.

param.name: pseudocount
param.label: Pseudocount
param.help: Add pseudocount to break ties and handle zeros in sparse RNA-seq data. Leave blank (default) for no regularization (pseudocount=0), or specify a numeric value between 0-10 for fixed pseudocount. Typical values: 0.5, 1.0. To use automatic estimation based on library size, set to a very small value like 0.001. Critical for rank-based non-parametric tests (e.g., Scheirer-Ray-Hare) which require unique orderings.

param.name: shrinkage
param.label: Shrinkage method
param.help: Shrinkage estimation for robust variance estimation. None: direct estimation. James-Stein: empirical Bayes shrinkage toward grand mean. Use James-Stein for small sample sizes or high-variance data.

param.name: min_valid_frac
param.label: Minimum valid fraction
param.help: Minimum fraction of valid (non-missing) values required to retain a transcript or gene (default: 0.75 = 75%). Transcripts with <75% valid samples are excluded due to insufficient data quality. Increase for stringent filtering; decrease to retain more transcripts.

param.name: reference_group
param.label: Reference group for normalization
param.help: (Optional) Reference group identifier for 'relative_reference' normalization method. Entropy values will be normalized relative to this group's mean. If empty, uses first condition group.

param.name: what_output
param.label: Diversity output type (advanced)
param.help: Output type for diversity calculation: 'S' = entropy (default) or 'D' = diversity (Hill numbers). Most users should use default entropy ('S'). Choose 'D' only if you specifically need diversity measures.

param.name: bootstrap
param.label: Enable bootstrap
param.help: Enable bootstrap resampling to compute robust confidence intervals for entropy estimates. Adds computational cost but provides non-parametric uncertainty quantification. Recommended for small sample sizes or non-normal data.

param.name: nboot
param.label: Bootstrap replicates
param.help: Number of bootstrap resampling iterations (default: 1000). Larger values (e.g., 5000) improve CI precision but increase runtime. Use ≥1000 for final analyses; 100-500 for exploratory work.

param.name: bootstrap_method
param.label: Bootstrap method
param.help: Bootstrap CI method. Percentile: simple, fast, less accurate for skewed distributions. BCa: bias-corrected & accelerated, more accurate and recommended for entropy estimates which may be skewed.

param.name: bootstrap_ci
param.label: Bootstrap CI level
param.help: Confidence level for CIs (0.95 = 95% CI). Narrower intervals (e.g., 0.90) are easier to achieve; wider intervals (e.g., 0.99) provide stronger confidence.

param.name: bootstrap_include_diagnostics
param.label: Include bootstrap diagnostics
param.help: Save bootstrap diagnostics including convergence checks, variance ratios, and CI coverage validations. Useful for assessing bootstrap stability and data quality.

param.name: sait_method
param.label: SAIT fitting method
param.help: Statistical method for Scale-Adaptive Interaction Testing (SAIT) of q × condition effects. GAM (recommended): Semi-parametric model with flexible smooth functions; best for non-linear q-curves. LMM: Linear mixed model with AR(1) correlation; good for repeated measures. FPCA: Functional PCA; reduces dimensionality for high q-resolution. GEE: Generalized estimating equations; robust to misspecified correlation structure.

param.name: sait_pcorr
param.label: SAIT p-value correction
param.help: Multiple testing correction for q × condition interaction tests (corrects for ~3089 transcripts). Hochberg: Step-up method, more powerful than Bonferroni, recommended. Benjamini-Hochberg: Controls FDR, most powerful for many tests. Bonferroni: Strict, very conservative. Holm: Step-down, better rejection power.

param.name: multicorr_method
param.label: Advanced multiple correction for SAIT
param.help: Advanced multiple comparison correction methods for SAIT interaction tests. Default uses primary p-value correction. Hochberg: More powerful than Bonferroni. Westfall-Young: Bootstrap-based, controls family-wise error rate. Benjamini-Yekutieli: Robust FDR control under dependence.

param.name: gee_corstr
param.label: GEE correlation structure
param.help: Correlation structure for Generalized Estimating Equations (only applicable if SAIT method is GEE). AR(1): Auto-regressive order 1 (good for q-spectrum). Exchangeable: treats all q-values as equally correlated. Independence: assumes independence (less rigid).

param.name: srh_advanced
param.label: Enable Scheirer-Ray-Hare advanced options
param.help: Enable advanced tuning for Scheirer-Ray-Hare rank-based test (Westfall-Young permutations, effect size thresholds, permutation bounds).

param.name: wy_randomizations
param.label: Westfall-Young randomizations
param.help: Number of permutations for Westfall-Young multiple testing correction (default: 500). Higher values improve statistical accuracy but increase computation time. Use 'auto' mode to estimate from data.

param.name: eta2_threshold_moderate
param.label: Moderate effect size threshold
param.help: Effect size (eta-squared) threshold for moderate effects (default: 0.01). Used to classify interaction strength: <0.01 = small, 0.01-0.1 = moderate, >0.1 = strong.

param.name: eta2_threshold_strong
param.label: Strong effect size threshold
param.help: Effect size (eta-squared) threshold for strong effects (default: 0.1). Classification: <0.01 = small, 0.01-0.1 = moderate, >0.1 = strong effects.

param.name: min_nperm
param.label: Minimum permutations
param.help: Minimum number of permutations for rank-based test (default: 100). Lower bound for adaptive permutation testing.

param.name: max_nperm
param.label: Maximum permutations
param.help: Maximum number of permutations for rank-based test (default: 10000). Upper bound for adaptive permutation testing.

param.name: jis_use_sait_fdr
param.label: Use SAIT FDR for jackknife switching detection
param.help: Use SAIT FDR threshold to filter genes before jackknife isoform switching analysis. Enables two-stage approach: (1) identify genes with significant q × condition interaction, (2) detect transcripts with robust switching patterns within those genes. Recommended for specificity.

param.name: jis_advanced
param.label: Enable JIS advanced options
param.help: Enable advanced tuning for Jackknife Isoform Switching analysis (p-value thresholds, outlier percentiles).

param.name: jis_sait_p_threshold
param.label: JIS SAIT p-value threshold
param.help: P-value threshold for filtering genes in JIS analysis via SAIT results (default: 0.05). Genes with uncorrected p-values below this threshold are included in jackknife switching detection. Lower values (e.g., 0.01) increase stringency.

param.name: jis_threshold
param.label: JIS outlier percentile threshold
param.help: Percentile threshold for outlier detection on isoform relative influences (default: 90). Isoforms with influence above this percentile are considered influential for switching detection. Higher values (e.g., 95) focus on strongly influential isoforms.

param.name: p_threshold
param.label: P-value threshold
param.help: Uncorrected p-value threshold for statistical tests. Typical: 0.05. Note: Used before multiple testing correction in parametric (SAIT) and rank-based (Scheirer-Ray-Hare) analyses.

param.name: fdr_threshold
param.label: FDR threshold
param.help: False Discovery Rate (FDR) adjusted p-value threshold for controlling Type I error across multiple tests. Benjamini-Hochberg correction recommended. Typical: 0.05. Genes with adjusted p < FDR threshold are considered significant at specified false discovery rate.

param.name: significance_threshold
param.label: General significance threshold
param.help: General significance level applied across all statistical tests when no specific correction is specified. Typical: 0.05 (5% Type I error rate). May also apply to interaction tests (q × condition effects).

param.name: divergence_ci
param.label: Divergence CI level
param.help: Confidence level for Tsallis divergence estimates measuring information-theoretic distance between conditions (default: 0.95 = 95% CI). Divergence measures scale-dependent differences in isoform complexity between groups.

param.name: enable_m_estimator
param.label: Enable M-estimator robust sample QC
param.help: Enable M-estimator based sample quality assessment to detect influential outlier samples that may skew analyses. M-estimation provides robust quality metrics independent of distributional assumptions.

param.name: q_min
param.label: Minimum q value
param.help: Minimum entropic index q. q=0 emphasizes rare isoforms (richness); q=1 gives Shannon entropy (balanced diversity); q→0 detects if diversity is maintained or collapsed across rare variants.

param.name: q_max
param.label: Maximum q value
param.help: Maximum entropic index q. q=2 emphasizes dominant isoforms (Simpson index); high q is robust to rare variants and detects dominance shifts. Examine q-curves showing how results change across scales.

param.name: q_step
param.label: q step size
param.help: Resolution of q-spectrum (smaller = more q-values, higher resolution curves, slower). Default 0.05 creates 41 q-values from 0 to 2. Smaller steps (e.g., 0.02) provide smoother q-curves for visualizing scale-dependent patterns.

param.name: save_intermediates
param.label: Save analysis object
param.help: Save the TSENATAnalysis object as RDS for downstream R analysis

param.name: skip_unmapped
param.label: Skip unmapped transcripts
param.help: Skip transcripts not found in the annotation file (recommended: enabled). If disabled, analysis will fail if unmapped transcripts are present in Salmon files.

data.name: divergence_spectrum_plot_top
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Top genes divergence spectrum

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**Purpose**
TSENAT (Tsallis Entropy Analysis Toolbox) quantifies isoform-usage complexity in RNA-seq data using Tsallis entropy, detecting coordinate isoform switching and splicing-driven complexity changes that standard differential expression tools (DESeq2, edgeR) miss. While traditional RNA-seq analysis measures transcript abundance changes, TSENAT measures the **diversity** of isoform distributions (entropy) at the gene level, revealing genes with coordinated or antagonistic isoform patterns and scale-dependent complexity changes across different conditions.
-----
.. class:: infomark
**Key Concept: Tsallis Entropy and Scale-Dependent Complexity**
Tsallis entropy uses a tuning parameter **q** (entropic index) that reveals scale-dependent complexity:
- **q = 0.5**: Focuses on rare isoforms (sensitive to low-abundance variants and isoform richness) - **q = 1.0**: Shannon entropy (balanced view across all isoforms, standard diversity measure) - **q = 2.0**: Focuses on dominant isoforms (Simpson index, sensitive to major isoform changes)
TSENAT computes a *spectrum* of entropy values (sweeping q from 0 to 2+) to characterize the full complexity landscape, enabling you to identify scale-dependent patterns where complexity changes are visible at some q-values but not others.
-----
.. class:: infomark
**Analysis Workflow**
TSENAT executes a comprehensive analysis pipeline:
- **Data Filtering**: Remove low-abundance transcripts based on stringency level (soft, medium, or severe) - **Diversity Calculation**: Compute Tsallis entropy across q-spectrum for each gene and condition - **Q-spectrum Visualization**: Plot diversity q-curves showing complexity patterns across q-values - **SAIT Parametric Testing**: Fit Scale-Adaptive Interaction Testing models (GAM, LMM, FPCA, or GEE) for q × condition effects - **Jackknife Isoform Switching**: Detect transcript-level switching patterns with jackknife resampling validation - **Rank-based Testing**: Scheirer-Ray-Hare non-parametric test for q × condition interaction with Westfall-Young correction - **Divergence Analysis**: Compute Tsallis divergence measuring information-theoretic distance between conditions - **Concordance Analysis**: Compare parametric (SAIT) vs rank-based (SRH) test agreement - **Quality Control**: M-estimator sample influence assessment and statistical assumptions validation - **Visualization**: Comprehensive plots including influence heatmaps, top transcripts patterns, and divergence distributions
-----
.. class:: infomark
**Input: Sample Sheet (Metadata File)**
TSENAT requires a tab-delimited sample sheet file containing sample information. You will provide this file and then select which columns correspond to sample IDs, experimental conditions, and pairing information (if applicable).
**File Format:**
The sample sheet should be a tab-separated text file with:
**Required columns:**
- A column for sample IDs (must match Salmon quantification file names exactly, case-sensitive, without .sf extension) - A column for experimental conditions/treatment groups (e.g., normal, tumor, control, treatment)
**Optional column:**
- A column for pairing/subject information (if using paired/repeated-measures designs; e.g., patient ID, animal ID)
**Column names are flexible** - you will select which column plays which role when you set up the tool parameters.
**Example sample sheet:**
sample condition paired_samples SRR14800481 normal A SRR14800480 normal B SRR14800479 tumor A SRR14800478 tumor B SRR14800485 normal C SRR14800486 tumor C
**Usage Instructions:**
1. Upload your sample sheet file with sample information 2. Select which column contains sample IDs (must match Salmon file names) 3. Select which column contains experimental conditions 4. If using paired design, select which column identifies paired samples
**Important:**
- Sample IDs in your selected column must match Salmon file names exactly (case-sensitive) - If sample names don't match, the tool will report a "Sample name mismatch" error - For paired sample analysis, each unique value in the pairing column should represent one subject/pair with multiple samples
